---
title: "The Efficient ASI Hypothesis"
chapter_id: "the-efficient-asi-hypothesis"
part_id: "stack-from-above"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "viea"
  - "moecot"
  - "rmi"
  - "cgs"
  - "rankfold_neuralfold"
  - "bbvca_v9"
  - "simulation_scaling"
  - "beastbrain"
  - "beastbrain_timeless"
  - "aletheia"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# The Efficient ASI Hypothesis

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `the-efficient-asi-hypothesis` |
| Part | Part I - The Stack from Above |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `viea`, `moecot`, `rmi`, `cgs`, `rankfold_neuralfold`, `bbvca_v9`, `simulation_scaling`, `beastbrain`, `beastbrain_timeless`, `aletheia` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The book needs a non-scale-only theory for why a governed ASI stack could become more capable without requiring every task to use maximal cognition.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Parameter count does not by itself explain routing, context selectivity, procedural memory, compression, verification, or runtime targeting.

## Core claim

[the-efficient-asi-hypothesis.core, support: argument] Efficient ASI is a design hypothesis: capability improves when cognition is routed, compressed, reused, governed, and tested.

## Mechanism

- Route subtasks to the smallest adequate specialist.
- Preserve reusable work as artifacts, tools, and procedural memory.
- Track residual burden when compression or routing leaves unresolved work.

## Interfaces

- Planning requests minimum viable intelligence.
- Routing selects specialists and tools.
- Evidence layers decide whether efficiency claims are supported.

## Invariants

- Efficiency claims require measured task quality and cost.
- Compression must expose residuals instead of hiding them.
- Fallback paths remain available when lightweight routes fail.

## Failure modes

- Treating cheap inference as sufficient evidence of intelligence.
- Compressing away critical context.
- Optimizing benchmark scores while increasing hidden residual burden.

## Minimal implementation

A costed task-routing model with explicit fallback, residual accounting, and benchmark gates.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Minimum viable intelligence routing test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Residual burden accounting test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Compression utility preservation test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |
| `rmi` | Ratcheting Modular Intelligence | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmark pressure, residual escrow, verified modular capability, regression preservation. |
| `cgs` | Compact Generative Systems | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Smallest adequate structure that can generate/govern target without hiding residual complexity. |
| `rankfold_neuralfold` | RankFold + NeuralFold | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Tensor/artifact compression. Low-rank residual coding plus functional preprocessing and probe-route fallback. |
| `bbvca_v9` | BBVCA_v9_final_public_release | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Prefer v9. Generate-verify-repair compression from seeded local laws, bounded search, two-phase rate discipline. |
| `simulation_scaling` | Simulation Scaling Law | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Resource constraints on scope, clockspeed, and fidelity in simulations. |
| `beastbrain` | BeastBrain Cognitive Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Architecture lineage. Organism paradigm, SSD-native/geometrically verified intelligence. |
| `beastbrain_timeless` | BeastBrain Architecture: Timeless Edition | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Definitive standalone master plan variant. |
| `aletheia` | Aletheia Foundry | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Safe general intelligence predecessor. Use ideas; avoid final branding if collision remains. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
